topic: AWS Strands Agents SDK: Build Production AI Agents in 2026

key_points: Official AWS open-source announcement post for Strands Agents SDK
Model-driven approach: describe goal, attach tools, agent decides at runtime
Works with Amazon Bedrock, Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, LiteLLM, Llama, Ollama, Writer
Ships in Python and TypeScript
Used internally by Amazon Q Developer, AWS Glue, VPC Reachability Analyzer
Strands is the easiest to start with — minimal API surface
LangGraph uses graph/state-machine approach (steeper learning curve)
CrewAI uses crew/role metaphor (intuitive for PMs)
Strands is best for fast prototyping with MCP tools
LangGraph is most battle-tested for complex deterministic workflows
Strands 1.0 adds Graph, Swarm, and Workflow multi-agent patterns
A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol for cross-framework interoperability
Session manager for remote state retrieval
Handoff primitive: pass control to human or other agent
Async support improved throughout SDK
Practical guide for deploying Strands + MCP tools to Lambda and Fargate
Lambda: serverless, pay-per-use, good for event-driven and short-lived agents
Fargate: container-based, supports streaming, good for long-running agents
Separate agent loop (Fargate) from tool execution (Lambda) for cost efficiency
Architecture: API Gateway → Fargate (agent loop) → Lambda (tools)
Built-in OpenTelemetry (OTEL) instrumentation
Integrates natively with AWS X-Ray and CloudWatch
Agent event loop emits traces, metrics, and logs
Tool execution, reasoning steps, model calls all instrumented
Supports fan-out telemetry routing to multiple backends

stats.fact: Strands Agents SDK reached 14 million+ downloads since its May 2025 launch
SDK hit 1 million downloads and 3,000+ GitHub stars within 4 months of preview launch
Verisk Analytics uses Strands SDK with Amazon Bedrock RAG to reduce MTTR by 60% without manual coding
Amazon internal teams (Amazon Q Developer, AWS Glue, VPC Reachability Analyzer) use Strands in production
Strands supports 9+ model providers: Amazon Bedrock, Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, LiteLLM, Llama, Ollama, Writer, and custom providers
Strands Agents 1.0 ships with Graph, Swarm, and Workflow multi-agent patterns plus A2A protocol support
Amazon Bedrock AgentCore provides production-grade deployment of Strands agents with built-in identity, memory, observability, and tool integration

angles: Minimal code paradigm: build a working AI agent in under 10 lines of Python — the lowest barrier to entry of any major agent framework
AWS-native production path: how Strands + Bedrock AgentCore eliminates infrastructure boilerplate for enterprise deployments
Model-agnostic architecture: use Bedrock, Anthropic, OpenAI, or local Ollama with a single API surface — no framework lock-in
MCP-first design: native Model Context Protocol support unlocks thousands of pre-built tools (databases, APIs, file systems) without custom integrations
Head-to-head: when to choose Strands vs LangGraph vs CrewAI — practical decision framework for 2026 teams
Observability-first: built-in OpenTelemetry means zero extra setup to get traces, metrics, and logs into CloudWatch/X-Ray/Grafana
outline: H2: What Is AWS Strands Agents SDK?
H2: Why Strands Agents? Key Advantages Over Competitors
H2: Installation and Quick Start (Python & TypeScript)
H2: Core Concepts: Tools, Model Providers, and the Agent Loop
H2: Built-in Tool Ecosystem and MCP Integration
H2: Multi-Agent Patterns: Graph, Swarm, and Workflow
H2: Deploying to Production: Lambda, Fargate, and Bedrock AgentCore
H2: Observability and Monitoring with OpenTelemetry
H2: Strands Agents vs LangGraph vs CrewAI: Which Should You Use?
H2: Real-World Use Cases and Enterprise Adoption
